Document:
# FIGHTER-CLASS ATTACK SHUTTLE
[color=#a4885c]Ship Size:[/color] Micro
[color=#a4885c]Fleet Shuttle:[/color] Yes
[color=#a4885c]Recommended Crew:[/color] 1
[color=#a4885c]Power Gen Type:[/color] APU
[color=#a4885c]Expeditions:[/color] No
[color=#a4885c]IFF Console:[/color] Yes
[color=#a4885c]Minimum Access Level:[/color] Deputy
"Micro attack vessel used for boarding and transport of prisoners."
# Tips on running NSF Fighter shuttles - The Fighter supports up to [color=#a4885c]12 [bold]kW[/bold][/color] on its shuttle APUs. It is not recommended to go above this due to the small interior space. For more general information on the power grid and other life support systems, refer to the [textlink="Power" link="Power"] guidebook article and the [textlink="Preflight Checklist" link="PreflightChecklist"/]. - For the basics of [bold]shuttle piloting[/bold], refer to [textlink="Piloting" link="Piloting"] and [textlink="Sector Topology" link="SectorTopology"/]. - For the basics of [bold]cargo hauling[/bold], refer to [textlink="Cargo Hauling" link="CargoHauling"]. - For the basics of [bold]expedition diving[/bold], refer to [textlink="Expeditions" link="Expeditions"]. - As a Captain of a Nanotrasen vessel, you have the authority to hire, fire, demote, or promote crew members at will, refer to [textlink="Hiring" link="Hiring"] for more information.
